profile.name: Digvijay Kumar Singh
profile.tagline: Building production-ready SaaS applications with Next.js, MERN, and AI integrations.
profile.intro: I build full-stack web applications focused on real-world product workflows, authentication, payments, AI integrations, and scalable backend architecture. My work centers around turning ideas into deployable products that go beyond tutorials and demonstrate practical engineering decisions.
profile.availability: Open to full-time roles, freelance projects, and product engineering opportunities.
journey: My learning approach has always been project-first. Instead of relying only on tutorials, I focus on building complete products from frontend interfaces to backend systems and deployments.
Over time, I moved from simple web applications to SaaS-style products involving authentication, billing, AI integrations, dashboards, and multi-module workflows.
Today, my focus is on building scalable web applications while improving backend architecture, product quality, and engineering depth.

timeline.title: Transitioned from foundational web development into structured full-stack application development using React, Node.js, and MongoDB.
timeline.description: Moved from foundational web practice into deeper React, backend, and deployment work with a stronger product-building mindset.

timeline.title: Built and deployed multiple production-style web applications while strengthening frontend architecture, backend systems, and deployment workflows.
timeline.description: Strengthened interface fundamentals, responsive design discipline, GitHub usage, and deployment confidence through consistent shipping.

timeline.title: Started building production-style MERN applications
timeline.description: Used JobPilot AI, DsSyncHub, and DevFlow AI to practice authentication, billing flows, API design, SaaS structure, and more intentional UI decisions.

timeline.title: Actively improving system design, backend engineering, and product development practices while pursuing software engineering opportunities.
timeline.description: Continuing with DSA, API architecture, deployment workflows, and cleaner frontends that feel calm, useful, and technically grounded.

projects.name: JobPilot AI
projects.label: AI job tracker
projects.summary: A full-stack job application tracker with analytics, reminders, kanban workflow, and AI-assisted follow-up support.
projects.description: JobPilot AI was built around a real problem: keeping a job search organized once applications, reminders, follow-ups, and status changes start spreading across notes and tabs. The product brings those moving pieces into one focused dashboard.
projects.challenge: Turn job tracking into a workflow-driven product instead of a basic CRUD list, while still handling authentication, reminders, analytics, and AI assistance without making the interface feel noisy.
projects.solution: Structured the product around a clean dashboard, protected auth flows, kanban-based status movement, analytics views, and email reminder support so the experience feels useful beyond a demo.
frontendDetails: Next.js application structure for a dashboard-first product
Tailwind-driven responsive cards, tables, and kanban surfaces
theme-aware interface patterns for a calmer working experience
backendDetails: Express API for authentication, jobs, reminders, and analytics logic
MongoDB-backed persistence for users, jobs, and workflow data
JWT-based protected routes and email handling with Nodemailer
architectureHighlights: workflow-oriented dashboard instead of simple form pages
deployment split across Vercel and Render

projects.name: DsSyncHub
projects.label: team workspace saas
projects.summary: A production-style collaboration platform for tasks, notes, workspace flows, billing, and team-oriented productivity modules.
projects.description: DsSyncHub pushes beyond a single module app. It brings workspace structure, task handling, notes, billing, and team-ready product thinking into one SaaS-style build designed to feel broader and more realistic.
projects.challenge: Build a multi-module product without letting the interface or architecture become fragmented, while also handling auth, global state, billing, and a more scalable frontend structure.
projects.solution: Used a typed React client, Redux-backed state handling, and modular backend routes so each feature could grow independently while still feeling like one coherent product.
highlights: task CRUD with status and priority flows
frontendDetails: React and TypeScript client with a more scalable component structure
Redux Toolkit for shared auth and application state
Tailwind-based SaaS interface across dashboard, billing, notes, and task views
backendDetails: Express API for auth, workspace flows, tasks, notes, billing, and account logic
MongoDB and Mongoose for product entities and user state
JWT security, Google OAuth integration, and Nodemailer support
architectureHighlights: typed frontend structure for a broader product surface
module-oriented SaaS thinking instead of a single-feature app
public deployment split between Vercel and Render

projects.name: DevFlow AI
projects.label: AI chat saas
projects.summary: A full-stack AI chat product with usage limits, billing, settings, and subscription-aware user flows.
projects.description: DevFlow AI focuses on a cleaner SaaS interaction model where chat, limits, billing, and account controls sit inside one cohesive product. It is less about novelty and more about handling several product concerns together.
projects.challenge: Blend AI chat interaction, usage restrictions, authentication, and billing into a simple experience that still feels product-ready.
projects.solution: Built a full-stack flow around chat persistence, guarded free-plan limits, upgrade paths through Razorpay, and settings-oriented account management backed by a separate API service.
highlights: daily usage limits for free plans
frontendDetails: Next.js frontend organized around chat, billing, and settings flows
Tailwind-based application UI with account-focused views
state management for user and plan-aware behavior
backendDetails: Express API for auth, chat persistence, usage checks, and billing logic
MongoDB-backed storage for user and subscription-related data
JWT-protected routes and payment integration through Razorpay
architectureHighlights: separate frontend and backend deployment model
AI interaction integrated into a broader SaaS structure

contact.heading: Let's build scalable products together.
contact.description: I am interested in software engineering opportunities, freelance projects, and collaborations focused on building high-quality web applications and modern digital products. Feel free to reach out if you want to connect, collaborate, or just chat about web development and product engineering.
